title: Eustephia cyrtanthoides
taxonomy: Eustephia cyrtanthoides is classified within the family Amaryllidaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as the lily family. This classification places Eustephia cyrtanthoides alongside other closely related species. The genus Eustephia is further grouped within the subfamily Allioideae, which contains species with onion-like characteristics. Within the Allioideae subfamily, Eustephia cyrtanthoides shares similarities with other genera such as Allium and Nothoscordum. This classification system helps to organize and understand the evolutionary relationships among these plant species.
preparations: Eustephia cyrtanthoides, also known as the yellow broom, is a plant with various traditional uses. However, I do not have information on specific herbal preparations from this plant. Eustephia cyrtanthoides is native to the Southern Hemisphere, particularly in Argentina, and its uses are not well-documented.
